subject: [PATCH v4 1/4] sunrpc: route to a populated pool in svc_pool_for_cpu()

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary:
NeilBrown noted that deriving sv_is_pooled from sv_nrpools is problematic due to the single-node case, and suggested removing sv_nrpools altogether since it implies a reference to svc_pool_map

Reviewer pointed out that deriving sv_is_pooled from sv_nrpools is not possible due to the single-node case, and suggested that pulling svc_pool_map.npools into cache during receive would be acceptable
reviews.raw_body:
Agree.  We have to keep sv_is_pooled (it implies a reference to
svc_pool_map).  I'm not convinced that we need sv_nrpools.

---

svc_pool_map is a single global that is read-mostly, so no dereference,
easy to predict.
During receive we need to access svc_pool_map anyway to map CPU to POOL.
So the svc_pool_map.npools will have to be pulled into cache anyway.

But if you don't like it, I won't push it on you.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch hardcodes the pool mode to per-node in the sunrpc code, effectively removing other modes. The change is necessary because it's not possible to derive whether a service is pooled or not based on the number of pools alone due to edge cases like single-node hosts. This cleanup simplifies the code and avoids potential performance implications from accessing the svc_pool_map during receives.
